name: Qwik
description: A resumable framework that delivers instant-loading web applications with O(1) JavaScript regardless of application size

description.value: A resumable framework that delivers instant-loading web applications with O(1) JavaScript regardless of application size

initial-release.comment: Initially introduced in 2021 by Misko Hevery (creator of AngularJS); beta v0.1.0 released October 2022; v1.0 released 2023

active-maintenance.comment: Very active development; 5,299 total commits, 626 contributors, releases in December 2024; v2.0 beta in development

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity with signals (useSignal, useStore); surgical component re-rendering

component-model.comment: Function components defined with component$() wrapper; $ suffix indicates lazy-loadable boundary

state-management-approach.comment: Comprehensive built-in state with useSignal, useStore, useComputed$, and context API

opinionatedness.comment: Provides strong conventions for resumability while allowing flexibility in architecture; similar to Next.js/Nuxt patterns

backward-compatibility.comment: Production-ready since v1.0; no expected breaking changes after three years of development; Builder.io uses in production

primary-language.comment: Core framework written in TypeScript (57%); optimizer written in Rust (3.9%)

compiles-to.comment: Compiles JSX to optimized JavaScript with automatic code-splitting at $ boundaries

typescript-support.comment: Written in TypeScript with full type definitions; first-class TypeScript support out of the box

template-syntax.comment: JSX/TSX syntax familiar to React developers; class instead of className for HTML attributes

styling-approach.comment: Supports useStyles$ for scoped CSS; also works with Tailwind, CSS modules, PostCSS

build-required.comment: Build step required for Qwik Optimizer to create lazy-loadable chunks; uses Vite

reactive-primitives.comment: useSignal() for primitive reactive state; useStore() for reactive objects with deep reactivity

computed-values.comment: useComputed$() for synchronous derived values; useResource$() for async computed values

two-way-binding.comment: bind:value and bind:checked attributes for two-way data binding on form inputs

event-handling.comment: onClick$, onInput$ etc.; $ suffix enables lazy-loading of event handlers; supports preventdefault: and stoppropagation: modifiers

conditional-rendering.comment: Ternary operators, logical AND, and if statements in JSX for conditional rendering

list-rendering.comment: Array.map() with unique key prop on first child element

component-slots.comment: <Slot /> component for content projection; supports named slots via q:slot attribute

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in Portal/Teleport component; can be implemented manually

suspense.comment: <Resource> component provides Suspense-like functionality with onPending, onResolved, onRejected states

error-boundaries.comment: Error boundaries supported; implemented as part of 1.0-rc0 milestone

context-provide-inject.comment: createContextId(), useContextProvider(), and useContext() for dependency injection without prop drilling

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: ref attribute for direct DOM element access; useVisibleTask$ for imperative DOM operations

official-router.comment: Qwik City provides file-system based routing integrated with the framework

router-name.comment: Qwik City is the meta-framework providing routing, SSR, and more

nested-routes.comment: Full support for nested layouts and routes via directory structure

dynamic-routes.comment: [paramName] for single-segment parameters; [...catchAll] for multi-segment catch-all routes

route-guards.comment: onRequest handlers in layout files for middleware/guards; predictable execution order

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Routes automatically lazy-loaded due to resumability architecture; no explicit configuration needed

scroll-restoration.comment: Automatic scroll restoration synchronized with browser history

built-in-state.comment: useSignal() and useStore() for local component state management

built-in-global-state.comment: Context API (useContextProvider/useContext) for app-wide state; stores can be shared via context

global-state-library.comment: Built-in context and stores sufficient for most cases; no separate official library needed

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Devtools in prototyping stage; qwik/json parser available for debugging serialized state in console

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; state serialized to HTML for resumability but not to storage

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not natively supported; devtools still in early stages

official-cli.comment: npm create qwik@latest for project scaffolding; interactive setup with starter templates

browser-devtools.comment: Browser devtools in prototyping stage; only console-based JSON parser currently available

vs-code-extension.comment: Qwik snippets extension available; Qwik City Link autocomplete extension for route completion

error-messages.comment: Good error messages; Qwik Optimizer catches issues at build time; clear component context

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs with tutorials, API reference, guides; interactive tutorial available

bundle-size-core.comment: ~1 kB for Qwikloader (initial JS required for interactivity); additional code lazy-loaded on interaction

bundle-size-full.comment: ~42.5 kB total if all code downloaded (rarely happens); actual eager JS stays near 0-4 kB due to resumability

virtual-dom.comment: No Virtual DOM; uses JSX but Qwik is fundamentally different from React under the hood

compile-time-optimization.comment: Qwik Optimizer (written in Rust) performs static analysis to create lazy-loadable chunks at $ boundaries

tree-shaking.comment: Automatic code-splitting means unused code is naturally excluded from initial bundle

code-splitting.comment: Automatic fine-grained code splitting at every $ boundary; no manual configuration needed

lazy-components.comment: component$() creates lazy-loadable component boundaries; core innovation of Qwik

ssr-capable.comment: Full SSR with resumability instead of hydration; instant interactivity without re-execution

streaming-ssr.comment: Supports streaming SSR; v2 expected to improve this further

partial-hydration.comment: Resumability is more granular than partial hydration; only interactive parts download/execute code

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Qwik's approach (resumability, lazy loading) doesn't translate well to synthetic benchmarks; designed for real-world performance

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~23,600 weekly downloads for @builder.io/qwik as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: Qwik UI (headless + styled), Flowbite Qwik; QwikReact allows using React component libraries

meta-framework.comment: Qwik City is the built-in meta-framework with SSR, file-based routing, and API routes

major-companies-using.comment: Builder.io uses Qwik in production; growing adoption among performance-focused teams

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created and sponsored by Builder.io; Misko Hevery (AngularJS creator) leads development

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Estimated ~500 questions; smaller community compared to established frameworks

job-market-demand.comment: Niche adoption; 4.1% developer usage in State of Frontend; 24.3% want to learn it

official-testing-library.comment: @builder.io/qwik/testing provides createDOM utility for component testing

testing-library-name.comment: Vitest is recommended test runner; createDOM from @builder.io/qwik/testing for component tests

component-testing.comment: createDOM utility for unit testing components; vitest-browser-qwik for browser-based testing

e2e-testing-integration.comment: playwright-ct-qwik plugin for Playwright component testing; works with standard E2E tools

mobile-framework.comment: Official Capacitor integration for native mobile apps; SSG mode works well with Capacitor

pwa-support.comment: No official PWA integration documented; feature request exists but not implemented

desktop-framework.comment: Can use with Tauri or Capacitor for desktop applications

learning-curve.comment: Familiar React-like syntax but requires understanding resumability, $ conventions, and serialization concepts

concepts-to-learn.comment: Resumability, $ suffix convention, Signals, Stores, Tasks (useTask$/useVisibleTask$), Slots, Context, Qwik City routing

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~3 minutes to scaffold with npm create qwik@latest and run first component

migration-path.comment: qwik-react allows embedding React components; gradual migration possible

incremental-adoption.comment: qwik-react enables React components in Qwik apps; can adopt incrementally
